title: `links init` refinements — one-shot flags, interactive mode, --edit
description: Enhance `links init` with three modes: one-shot (all config via flags, no prompts), interactive (prompt for each field, all skippable), and edit (open existing or freshly scaffolded links.yaml in $EDITOR). Existing --force behaviour is preserved.

features: ONE-SHOT MODE: if any of --name, --bio, --theme, --link are provided, run non-interactively. Create links.yaml from the provided flags and exit. No prompts.
--theme <string>: sets the theme field (validate against listThemes() — exit 1 if unknown)

--link <label,url>: adds a link. Format is 'label,url' (comma-separated). Flag is repeatable: --link 'GitHub,https://github.com/you' --link 'Twitter,https://x.com/you'. Parse by splitting on first comma only. Validate url with validateUrl() — exit 1 if invalid.
If links.yaml already exists and --force is not passed, exit 1 with existing message.
INTERACTIVE MODE: default when no one-shot flags are provided and --edit is not passed.

Use node:readline to prompt for each field sequentially: name (default: system username), bio (default: empty, skippable), theme (default: minimal-dark, show available options), domain (skippable), avatar (skippable).
After profile fields: ask 'Add a link? (label,url or Enter to skip)' in a loop — repeat until user submits empty input. Each entry parsed same as --link flag. Validate each URL before accepting.
On completion: write links.yaml and print success + next steps.
If links.yaml exists: open it directly in $EDITOR (fallback: $VISUAL → nano → vi).
If links.yaml does not exist: scaffold it first (same as default init with system username default), then open it.
All three modes respect --force for overwrite protection.
Update src/commands/init.js in-place — no new files needed.

notes: readline prompts: use readline.createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout }). Wrap question() in a Promise for async/await usage. Close the interface when done or it will hang. --link parsing: split on first comma only — url may contain commas in theory. Interactive mode only triggers when zero one-shot flags are present AND --edit is absent.
